weather_line: Amber clearing to green · brass instruments unreliable · ducks expected

stories.headline: Duck Bandit Escalates to Phase Three: A Third Duck Appears Before Dawn; It Has a Note Attached; the Note Is in Code
body: At four fifty-three this morning, a third rubber duck was placed on the Jeep's bonnet. It is red. It is positioned six inches left of the green duck, maintaining the established interval with a precision that rules out coincidence and raises the question of instruments. Attached to its left foot — secured with a loop of twine — is a small square of parchment. The parchment contains seven characters. No one has yet determined whether they are letters, numbers, or something that is neither.

The sequence is now amber, green, red. Christine identified the progression at first light and described it, in a tone her Gringotts colleagues would recognise immediately, as 'consistent with a system.' Steve, who has had a hypothesis since Thursday and has not spoken it aloud, looked at the note for thirty seconds and said: 'That's what I thought.' He has still not elaborated. The brass ducks on the shelf shifted two further degrees overnight, and are now facing a direction for which the household compass has no marking.

The Ministry confirmed this morning that the situation remains 'under observation.' Weasleys' Wizard Wheezes has been contacted regarding the red shade; a spokesperson confirmed the colour exists but declined to specify whether it is currently in production, discontinued, or something else. The Prophet considers this the most interesting answer it has received all week. The Jeep has not been moved. Both Steve and Christine have looked at it from the northeast corner of the property, which is either investigative or habitual at this point, and possibly both.

stories.headline: Saturday Morning Reaches the Kitchen Table Without an Agenda; This Is Noted and Permitted; the Table Is Grateful
body: For the first time since the Hempworth chart was unfolded upon it on Wednesday the ninth, the kitchen table has nothing on it. No chart, no notations, no brass ducks mid-rotation, no Accord sub-clauses printed and annotated in three colours. This morning it holds only a cup of tea and a patch of Saturday light, which arrived through the east window at seven twelve and has not been asked to do anything in particular.

A household source described the atmosphere as 'settled.' The tea was described, separately, as correct. No meeting has been called. No arc is pending resolution. The table, for its part, is said to be taking the morning one moment at a time, which is either wisdom or the absence of anything requiring urgency, and on a Saturday these are the same.

stories.headline: Duty Goblin Attempts Saturday Correspondence; Is Reminded That It Is Saturday; Files the Reminder Under 'Disputed'
body: Gringotts Bank issued a brief internal memorandum this morning confirming that Saturday exists and that the Liaison Wing is not expected to respond to correspondence received between the hours of six and six on days ending in '-urday.' The memorandum was addressed to the duty goblin, who has received it, logged it, and filed a formal query regarding its jurisdictional basis. The query is expected to be reviewed on Monday.

Christine, reached for comment via a source close to the household's east window, said nothing, because it is Saturday and the tea is correct, and some things do not require a response. The duty goblin's query has been assigned a reference number. The reference number begins with a seven, which the goblin considers significant. It is not.

stories.headline: Ministry Issues Saturday Bulletin Confirming the Week Has Concluded; Describes It as 'A Week That Occurred'; Attaches the Red Duck as Exhibit A Without Explaining How It Obtained a Photograph
body: The Ministry of Magic's Office of Weekly Summation released its Saturday bulletin at nine o'clock, confirming that the week beginning Monday the fourteenth has now ended and that its events are, in the Ministry's assessment, 'on the record.' The bulletin notes the Duck Bandit development, the resolution of the Northeast by Nightfall expedition, the continued good standing of the Liaison Wing, and the ongoing contested status of the warmest lap. It describes the week overall as 'eventful in ways that do not require immediate action,' which the Prophet understands to mean the Ministry is pleased and will not be admitting it.

Attached to the bulletin is a photograph. The photograph shows the three ducks on the Jeep's bonnet, taken from an angle that does not correspond to any position a Ministry official has publicly acknowledged occupying this week. The caption reads: 'Exhibit A — for reference.' No further explanation is offered. The Ministry considers this sufficient.

familiars_corner: Lucy: Was observed this morning at a careful distance from the three ducks, studying the coded note with an expression that several witnesses described as recognition. She has not been asked about this directly. No one is ready for that conversation.

Ellie: Spotted the red duck at sunrise, approached it with great warmth, introduced herself, and waited for a response. After a reasonable interval she decided the duck was simply the quiet type and moved on. She has checked back twice.

Jersey: Having identified the patch of Saturday light on the kitchen table as the warmest available surface, filed a formal occupancy claim at seven-fourteen, was gently relocated, and is now reconstituting his position one inch at a time with a patience that can only be described as geological.
founding_editor_note: Seven characters in a code on parchment tied to a duck — I built the press that printed the alphabet, and I'll tell you, whoever laid that twine used a proper square knot, which is more than I can say for the bracket on the third-floor archive shelf. — D.

in_brief.sport: Saturday Strongwoman Rankings remain unchanged; the only competition is Monday, and Monday has not yet qualified.
in_brief.forecast: Settled through midmorning · a fifth character of the code may become legible by afternoon · the warmest lap dispute will enter its third week regardless of conditions.

storyline_updates.title: The Duck Bandit Stirs Again
storyline_updates.summary: Day 3: A third duck appeared at four fifty-three, red, maintaining the established six-inch interval. A coded note — seven characters, method of encoding unknown — was attached to its left foot with twine. The sequence is amber, green, red. Christine has identified it as a system. Steve's unspoken hypothesis was confirmed to his own satisfaction upon seeing the note, but he has still not elaborated. The brass ducks are now facing a direction the household compass does not name. Weasleys' confirmed red exists but declined to say more. The Ministry has somehow obtained a photograph. Day 4 should bring either a partial decode of the note (perhaps one or two characters resolved, pointing somewhere or someone), Steve's hypothesis spoken aloud at last, or a fourth duck — one ahead of the expected sequence — that breaks the pattern in a way that reframes everything that came before. The direction the ducks face, and Lucy's apparent recognition of the code, are both live threads.
